---
title: "03_modeling_clinicalevent"
# output: html_document
---

# Environment

```{r}
library(skimr)
library(mgcv)
library(parallel)
library(parglm)
library(pROC)
library(ggplot2)
library(tibble)
library(dplyr)
library(feather)
library(broom)
library(htmlTable)
library(caret)
library(kableExtra)
library(magrittr)
# library(webshot2)
```

# Load File

```{r}
final_dataset <- (
  readRDS(
    "~/projects/vacunas_brands_2025/Rfiles/final_dataset_brands.rds"
  )
)

print(nrow(final_dataset))

# Filtrar los pacientes cuya edad es mayor a 100 años
final_dataset %<>%
  filter(age < 100)

print(paste("Después de eliminar edad >100:", nrow(final_dataset)))

# Filter up to percentile 95 in ultima_inmun_infecc_dias

summary(final_dataset$ultima_inmun_infecc_dias)

# Calcular el percentil 95 
p95 <- quantile(final_dataset$ultima_inmun_infecc_dias, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE)

# Filtrar el dataset eliminando los valores por encima del percentil 96
final_dataset <- final_dataset %>% 
  filter(is.na(ultima_inmun_infecc_dias) | ultima_inmun_infecc_dias <= p95)
print(paste("Después de eliminar ultima_inmun_infecc_dias > p95:", nrow(final_dataset)))
summary(final_dataset$ultima_inmun_infecc_dias)

# final_dataset <- final_dataset[1:200000,]

library(dplyr)

final_dataset <- final_dataset %>%
  mutate(
    age_grouped = case_when(
      age >= 12 & age <= 30 ~ "12-30",
      age > 30 & age <= 65 ~ "31-65",
      age > 65 ~ ">65",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    age_grouped = factor(age_grouped, levels = c("12-30", "31-65", ">65"))
  )
table(final_dataset$age_grouped)

final_dataset <- final_dataset %>%
  mutate(
    ultima_inmun_infecc_grouped = case_when(
      ultima_inmun_infecc_dias >= 14 & ultima_inmun_infecc_dias <= 45 ~ "14-45",
      ultima_inmun_infecc_dias > 45 & ultima_inmun_infecc_dias <= 90 ~ "46-90",
      ultima_inmun_infecc_dias > 90 & ultima_inmun_infecc_dias <= 170 ~ "91-170",
      ultima_inmun_infecc_dias > 170 ~ ">170",
      TRUE ~ "None"
    ),
    ultima_inmun_infecc_grouped = factor(ultima_inmun_infecc_grouped, levels = c("14-45", "None", "46-90", "91-170", ">170"))
  )
table(final_dataset$ultima_inmun_infecc_grouped)

saveRDS(final_dataset, file = "~/projects/vacunas_brands_2025/Rfiles/final_dataset_brands_excluded.rds")

170-250

>250



```

# Vectors/Dictionaries
```{r}

dict_terms <- c(
  "age" = "Age",
  "age_grouped12-30" = "Age (12-30)",
  "age_grouped31-65" = "Age (31-65)",
  "age_grouped>65" = "Age (>65)",
  "ultima_inmun_infecc_grouped14-45" = "Last immunization: 14-45 days",
  "ultima_inmun_infecc_groupedNone" = "No previous immunization",
  "ultima_inmun_infecc_grouped46-90" = "Last immunization: 46-90 days",
  "ultima_inmun_infecc_grouped91-170" = "Last immunization: 91-170 days",
  "ultima_inmun_infecc_grouped>170" = "Last immunization: >170 days",
  "desc_sexoHombre" = "Sex (male)",
  "variant_periodα" = "Period of α predominance",
  "variant_periodα/Δ transition" = "α-Δ transition period",
  "variant_periodΔ" = "Period of Δ predominance",
  "variant_periodΔ/Ό transition" = "Δ-Ό transition period",
  "variant_periodΌ" = "Period of Ό predominance",
  "asmaSí" = "Asthma",
  "cardiopatia_isquemicaSí" = "Ischemic heart disease",
  "cirrosis_hepaticaSí" = "Hepatic cirrhosis",
  "diabetesSí" = "Diabetes",
  "epocSí" = "Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD)",
  "hepatopatia_cronica_excepto_cirrosisSí" = "Chronic Hepatic Disease (except cirrhosis)",
  "hipertensionSí" = "Hypertension",
  "insuficiencia_cardiacaSí" = "Heart failure",
  "insuficiencia_renal_cronicaSí" = "Chronic kidney disease",
  "vihSí" = "HIV+",
  "cancer_hematologicoSí" = "Haematologic cancer",
  "cancer_organo_solidoSí" = "Solid organ cancer",
  "ultima_inmun_infecc_binNone" = "No vaccination/infection",
  "ultima_inmun_infecc_bin31-60" = "Last vaccination/infection (31-60 days ago)",
  "ultima_inmun_infecc_bin61-90" = "Last vaccination/infection (61-90 days ago)",
  "ultima_inmun_infecc_bin91-120" = "Last vaccination/infection (91-120 days ago)",
  "ultima_inmun_infecc_bin121-180" = "Last vaccination/infection (121-180 days ago)",
  "ultima_inmun_infecc_bin>180" = "Last vaccination/infection (>180 days ago)",
  "vac_1shotAstraZeneca" = "AstraZeneca",
  "vac_1shotJanssen" = "Janssen",
  "vac_1shotModerna" = "Moderna",
  "vac_1shotPfizer/BioNTech" = "Pfizer/BioNTech",
  "vac_1shotOther" = "Other",
  "vac_2shotAstraZeneca+AstraZeneca" = "AstraZeneca+AstraZeneca",
  "vac_2shotModerna+Moderna" = "Moderna+Moderna",
  "vac_2shotPfizer/BioNTech+Pfizer/BioNTech" = "Pfizer/BioNTech+Pfizer/BioNTech",
  "vac_2shotOther" = "Other",
  "vac_3shotAstraZeneca+AstraZeneca+Moderna" = "AstraZeneca+AstraZeneca+Moderna",
  "vac_3shotAstraZeneca+AstraZeneca+Pfizer/BioNTech" = "AstraZeneca+AstraZeneca+Pfizer/BioNTech",
  "vac_3shotModerna+Moderna+Moderna" = "Moderna+Moderna+Moderna",
  "vac_3shotPfizer/BioNTech+Pfizer/BioNTech+Moderna" = "Pfizer/BioNTech+Pfizer/BioNTech+Moderna",
  "vac_3shotPfizer/BioNTech+Pfizer/BioNTech+Pfizer/BioNTech" = "Pfizer/BioNTech+Pfizer/BioNTech+Pfizer/BioNTech"
)

  # Variable mapping and ordering
variable_order <- rev(c(
"Intercept", "Age", 

"Age (12-30)", "Age (31-65)", "Age (>65)", 
    
"AstraZeneca", "Janssen", "Moderna", "Pfizer/BioNTech",
    
"AstraZeneca+AstraZeneca", "Moderna+Moderna", "Pfizer/BioNTech+Pfizer/BioNTech",
    
"AstraZeneca+AstraZeneca+Moderna", "AstraZeneca+AstraZeneca+Pfizer/BioNTech", "Moderna+Moderna+Moderna",
"Pfizer/BioNTech+Pfizer/BioNTech+Moderna", "Pfizer/BioNTech+Pfizer/BioNTech+Pfizer/BioNTech", 
    
"Last immunization: 14-45 days", "No previous immunization", "Last immunization: 46-90 days", "Last immunization: 91-170 days", "Last immunization: >170 days"
  ))
  
# Independent variables (no spline)
predictor_vars <- c("desc_sexo", "variant_period", "asma", "cardiopatia_isquemica", "cirrosis_hepatica", "diabetes", "epoc", "hepatopatia_cronica_excepto_cirrosis", "hipertension", "insuficiencia_cardiaca", "insuficiencia_renal_cronica", "vih", "cancer_hematologico", "cancer_organo_solido", "ultima_inmun_infecc_bin")
# Independent variables (spline)
spline_vars <- c("age")

```

# Functions

## Extract OR
```{r}
# Extracts and formats logistic regression model coefficients into a data frame with OR and CI
extract_coef_pval_OR <- function(model) {
  
  as.data.frame(tibble(term = names(model$coefficients),
                       estimate = model$coefficients,
                       std.error = sqrt(diag(vcov(model))),
                       statistic = model$coefficients / sqrt(diag(vcov(model))),
                       p.value = 2 * (1 - pnorm(abs(statistic))),
                       OR = round(exp(estimate),2),
                       OR_CI_lower = round(exp(estimate - 1.96 * std.error),2),
                       OR_CI_upper = round(exp(estimate + 1.96 * std.error),2)
  )
  )
} 

```

## GAM model with 1 spline (age)
```{r}

# Function for Logistic Regression Analysis
log_reg_gam_1s <- function(data, vaccine_variable, file_name, title) {
  
  print('Logistic Regression of Clinical Event')
  start_time <- Sys.time() 
  print(Sys.time())
  
# Construir fórmula dinámicamente
  formula_text <- paste0( 
    "clinical_event ~ s(age) + ultima_inmun_infecc_grouped + ", vaccine_variable, " + desc_sexo + variant_period + asma + cardiopatia_isquemica + cirrosis_hepatica + diabetes + epoc + hepatopatia_cronica_excepto_cirrosis + hipertension + insuficiencia_cardiaca + insuficiencia_renal_cronica + vih + cancer_hematologico + cancer_organo_solido"
    )
formula_glm <- as.formula(formula_text)

  
  ## Fit logistic regression model
  model <- gam(
  formula = formula_glm,
  data = data,
  family = binomial(), 
  method = "REML"
)
  
  print(summary(model))
  
  ## Extract odds ratios
  model_df_1 <- extract_coef_pval_OR(model)
  
  model_df_2 <- model_df_1 %>%
    mutate(
      term = case_when(
        term == "(Intercept)" ~ "Intercept",
        TRUE ~ coalesce(dict_terms[term], term)  # Use dictionary mapping
      ),
      term = factor(term, levels = variable_order),
      adverse_protective = case_when(
        OR_CI_lower > 1 & OR_CI_upper > 1 ~ "Adverse",
        OR_CI_lower < 1 & OR_CI_upper < 1 ~ "Protective",
        TRUE ~ "Inconclusive"
      ),
      adverse_protective = factor(adverse_protective, levels = c("Adverse", "Protective", "Inconclusive"))
    ) %>%
    filter(term != "Intercept")
  
  ## Save OR table
  write.csv(
    model_df_2 %>%
      arrange(factor(term, levels = rev(variable_order))),
    paste0("~/projects/vacunas_brands_2025/data/OR_", file_name, ".csv"),
    row.names = FALSE
  )
  
  ## Generate HTML table
  html_output <- htmlTable(
    model_df_2 %>%
      select(term, OR, OR_CI_lower, OR_CI_upper, adverse_protective) %>%
      mutate(term = factor(term, levels = variable_order)) %>%
      arrange(factor(term, levels = rev(variable_order))),
  caption = paste0(title, " - Odds Ratio and Confidence Intervals for composite outcome-associated Factors")
)
  print(html_output)
  
  ## Create the plot with ggplot2
  plot <- ggplot(model_df_2, 
                 aes(x = OR, xmin = OR_CI_lower, xmax = OR_CI_upper, y = term, color = adverse_protective)) +
    geom_point(size = 2, na.rm = TRUE) +  
    geom_errorbarh(height = 0.2, na.rm = TRUE) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed") +
    labs(x = "Odds Ratio", y = NULL, title = paste0("Logistic Regression. Odds Ratio and Confidence Intervals \nfor Composite Outcome-associated Factors.\n", title)) +
    theme_minimal() +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c("Adverse" = "#c0392b", "Protective" = "#27ae60", "Inconclusive" = "gray70"),
      limits = c("Adverse", "Protective", "Inconclusive"), #ensure all levels appear in legend
      labels = c("Adverse", "Protective", "Inconclusive"),
      name = "Effect on Outcome",
      guide = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3)) 
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 5)) +
    theme(
      plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
      panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
      plot.title.position = "plot",
      legend.position = "top",
      axis.ticks = element_blank(),
      axis.text.x = element_text(color = "#2c3e50"),
      axis.text.y = element_text(color = "#2c3e50"))
  
  print(plot)

  ## Save plot
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("~/projects/vacunas_brands_2025/figures/Forest_plot/fp_gam_1spline_", file_name, ".png"),
    plot = plot, 
    width = 8, 
    height = 6, 
    dpi = 300
  )
  
   time_elapsed <- round(difftime(Sys.time(), start_time, units = "mins"), 2)
  print(paste("Time to run:", time_elapsed, "minutes"))
}


```


## GAM model with 2 splines
```{r}

# Function for Logistic Regression Analysis
log_reg_gam_2s <- function(data, vaccine_variable, file_name, title, number_splines) {
  
  print('Logistic Regression of Clinical Event')
  start_time <- Sys.time() 
  print(Sys.time())
  
  ## Change independent variables for spline of age + last immunization days
  if (number_splines == 2) {
    # Add 'ultima_inmun_infecc_dias' to spline_vars
    spline_vars <- c("age", "ultima_inmun_infecc_dias")
    # And remove 'ultima_inmun_infecc_bin' from predictor_vars
    predictor_vars <- predictor_vars[-which(predictor_vars == "ultima_inmun_infecc_bin")]
  }
  
  ## Define spline terms
  spline_terms <- paste0("s(", spline_vars, ")")
  # Combine predictor variables with spline terms
  all_predictors <- c(spline_terms, predictor_vars)
  # Create formula
  formula <- as.formula(paste("clinical_event", "~", paste(all_predictors, collapse = " + "), " + ", vaccine_variable))
  
  ## Fit logistic regression model
  model <- gam(
    formula,
    data = data,
    family = binomial(),
    method = "REML"
  )
  
  print(summary(model))
  
  ## Extract odds ratios
  model_df_1 <- extract_coef_pval_OR(model)
  
  model_df_2 <- model_df_1 %>%
    mutate(
      term = case_when(
        term == "(Intercept)" ~ "Intercept",
        TRUE ~ coalesce(dict_terms[term], term)  # Use dictionary mapping
      ),
      term = factor(term, levels = variable_order),
      adverse_protective = case_when(
        OR_CI_lower > 1 & OR_CI_upper > 1 ~ "Adverse",
        OR_CI_lower < 1 & OR_CI_upper < 1 ~ "Protective",
        TRUE ~ "Inconclusive"
      ),
      adverse_protective = factor(adverse_protective, levels = c("Adverse", "Protective", "Inconclusive"))
    ) %>%
    filter(term != "Intercept")
  
  ## Save OR table
  write.csv(
    model_df_2 %>%
      arrange(factor(term, levels = rev(variable_order))),
    paste0("~/projects/vacunas_brands_2025/data/OR_", file_name, ".csv"),
    row.names = FALSE
  )
  
  ## Generate HTML table
  html_output <- htmlTable(
    model_df_2 %>%
      select(term, OR, OR_CI_lower, OR_CI_upper, adverse_protective) %>%
      mutate(term = factor(term, levels = variable_order)) %>%
      arrange(factor(term, levels = rev(variable_order))),
  caption = paste0(title, " - Odds Ratio and Confidence Intervals for composite outcome-associated Factors")
)
  print(html_output)
  
  ## Create the plot with ggplot2
  plot <- ggplot(model_df_2, 
                 aes(x = OR, xmin = OR_CI_lower, xmax = OR_CI_upper, y = term, color = adverse_protective)) +
    geom_point(size = 2, na.rm = TRUE) +  
    geom_errorbarh(height = 0.2, na.rm = TRUE) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed") +
    labs(x = "Odds Ratio", y = NULL, title = paste0("Logistic Regression. Odds Ratio and Confidence Intervals \nfor Composite Outcome-associated Factors.\n", title)) +
    theme_minimal() +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c("Adverse" = "#c0392b", "Protective" = "#27ae60", "Inconclusive" = "gray70"),
      limits = c("Adverse", "Protective", "Inconclusive"), #ensure all levels appear in legend
      labels = c("Adverse", "Protective", "Inconclusive"),
      name = "Effect on Outcome",
      guide = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3)) 
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 5)) +
    theme(
      plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
      panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
      plot.title.position = "plot",
      legend.position = "top",
      axis.ticks = element_blank(),
      axis.text.x = element_text(color = "#2c3e50"),
      axis.text.y = element_text(color = "#2c3e50"))
  
  print(plot)

  ## Save plot
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("~/projects/vacunas_brands_2025/figures/Forest_plot/fp_gam_2spline_", file_name, ".png"),
    plot = plot, 
    width = 8, 
    height = 6, 
    dpi = 300
  )
  
   time_elapsed <- round(difftime(Sys.time(), start_time, units = "mins"), 2)
  print(paste("Time to run:", time_elapsed, "minutes"))
}

```

## GAM plot

```{r}
# Definir la función para ajustar el modelo GAM y crear el gráfico
generate_gam_plot <- function(data, vaccine_variable, spline_var) {
  # Ajuste del modelo GAM
  print('GAM linear regression of Clinical Event')
  start_time <- Sys.time()
  print(Sys.time())

  # Definir el orden dinámico de las smooths
  if (spline_var == "age") {
    order_smooth <- "s(age) + s(ultima_inmun_infecc_dias)"
  } else if (spline_var == "ultima_inmun_infecc_dias") {
    order_smooth <- "s(ultima_inmun_infecc_dias) + s(age)"
  } else {
    stop("spline_var debe ser 'age' o 'ultima_inmun_infecc_dias'")
  }

  # Crear la fórmula completa como texto
  formula_text <- paste0(
    "clinical_event ~ ",
    order_smooth, " + ",
    vaccine_variable, " + desc_sexo + variant_period + asma + cardiopatia_isquemica + ",
    "cirrosis_hepatica + diabetes + epoc + hepatopatia_cronica_excepto_cirrosis + hipertension + ",
    "insuficiencia_cardiaca + insuficiencia_renal_cronica + vih + cancer_hematologico + cancer_organo_solido"
  )

  # Convertir a fórmula
  formula_gam <- as.formula(formula_text)

  # Ajustar modelo
  model <- gam(
    formula = formula_gam,
    data = data,
    family = binomial(),
    method = "REML"
  )

  print(summary(model))

  # # Imprimir los nudos para las variables spline
  # print("Nudos para 'ultima_inmun_infecc_dias':")
  # print(model$smooth[[1]]$xp)  # Puntos de predicción (equivalente a nudos)
  #
  # print("Knots si están definidos explícitamente:")
  # print(model$smooth[[1]]$knots)
  #
  # print("Nombre de la variable spline:")
  # print(model$smooth[[1]]$term)


  # Función para extraer los datos del modelo GAM y calcular probabilidades
  extract_gam_data <- function(model, spline_var) {
    gam_plot_data <- plot(model, term = spline_var, pages = 1)[[1]] # Use term argument
    x <- gam_plot_data$x
    fit <- gam_plot_data$fit
    se <- gam_plot_data$se

    prob <- exp(fit) / (1 + exp(fit)) * 100
    lower <- exp(fit - 2 * se) / (1 + exp(fit - 2 * se)) * 100
    upper <- exp(fit + 2 * se) / (1 + exp(fit + 2 * se)) * 100

    data.frame(spline_var = x, probability = prob, lower = lower, upper = upper)
  }

# Función para crear el gráfico GAM
  create_gam_plot <- function(data, title, spline_var) {
    ggplot(data, aes(x = spline_var, y = probability)) +
      geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), alpha = 0.3) +
      geom_line() +
      labs(title = title, x = spline_var, y = "Probability (%)") +
      ylim(0, 100) +
      scale_x_continuous(breaks = scales::breaks_width(10)) +
      theme_minimal()
  }

  # Generar los datos para el gráfico y crear el gráfico
  plot_data <- extract_gam_data(model, spline_var)
  plot <- create_gam_plot(plot_data, paste0("GAM Plot for ", spline_var, " in the model ", vaccine_variable), spline_var)
  print(plot)

  # Guardar el gráfico
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("~/projects/vacunas_brands_2025/figures/GAM_plot/GAM_", vaccine_variable, "_2spline_", spline_var, ".png"),
    plot = plot,
    width = 8,
    height = 6,
    dpi = 300
  )

  time_elapsed <- round(difftime(Sys.time(), start_time, units = "mins"), 2)
  print(paste("Time to run:", time_elapsed, "minutes"))
}

```

# Forest plot - GAM - 1 spline (age)

## vac_1shot
```{r}
log_reg_gam_1s(
  data = final_dataset, 
  vaccine_variable = "vac_1shot", 
  file_name = "vac1shot",
  title = "1-shot Regimen"
  )

```

## vac_2shot
```{r}
log_reg_gam_1s(
  data = final_dataset, 
  vaccine_variable = "vac_2shot",
  file_name = "vac2shot",
  title = "2-shot Regimen"
  )

```

## vac_3shot
```{r}
log_reg_gam_1s(
  data = final_dataset, 
  vaccine_variable = "vac_3shot",
  file_name = "vac3shot",  
  title = "3-shot Regimen"
  )

```

# Forest plot - GAM - 2 splines

## vac_1shot
```{r}
log_reg_gam_2s(
  data = final_dataset, 
  vaccine_variable = "vac_1shot", 
  file_name = "vac1shot",
  title = "1-shot Regimen - Splines for Age and Last Immunization", 
  number_splines = 2)

```

## vac_2shot
```{r}
log_reg_gam_2s(
  data = final_dataset, 
  vaccine_variable = "vac_2shot",
  file_name = "vac2shot",
  title = "2-shot Regimen - Splines for Age and Last Immunization", 
  number_splines = 2)

```

## vac_3shot
```{r}
log_reg_gam_2s(
  data = final_dataset, 
  vaccine_variable = "vac_3shot",
  file_name = "vac3shot",  
  title = "3-shot Regimen - Splines for Age and Last Immunization", 
  number_splines = 2)

```


# GAM Plot of last immunization

## vac_1shot
```{r}
generate_gam_plot(data = final_dataset,
                  vaccine_variable = "vac_1shot",
                  spline_var = "ultima_inmun_infecc_dias")
```

## vac_2shot
```{r}
generate_gam_plot(data = final_dataset,
                  vaccine_variable = "vac_2shot",
                  spline_var = "ultima_inmun_infecc_dias")
```


## vac_3shot
```{r}
generate_gam_plot(data = final_dataset,
                  vaccine_variable = "vac_3shot",
                  spline_var = "ultima_inmun_infecc_dias")
```

# GAM Plot of age

## vac_1shot
```{r}
generate_gam_plot(data = final_dataset,
                  vaccine_variable = "vac_1shot",
                  spline_var = "age")
```

## vac_2shot
```{r}
generate_gam_plot(data = final_dataset,
                  vaccine_variable = "vac_2shot",
                  spline_var = "age")
```


## vac_3shot
```{r}
generate_gam_plot(data = final_dataset,
                  vaccine_variable = "vac_3shot",
                  spline_var = "age")
```
